questions.question: Write a C program to reverse a singly linked list iteratively.
keyPoints: Use three pointers: prev, current, next
Initialize prev to NULL
Traverse list, reversing each link
Update head to prev at end
Time: O(n), Space: O(1)

questions.question: Explain how to find the middle element of a singly linked list in one pass.
keyPoints: Use slow and fast pointer technique
Slow moves one step, fast moves two steps
When fast reaches end, slow is at middle
Time: O(n), Space: O(1)
For even length, returns second middle

questions.question: Write a function to detect if a linked list has a cycle. Explain the algorithm.
keyPoints: Floyd's Cycle Detection (Tortoise and Hare)
Two pointers: slow (1 step) and fast (2 steps)
If they meet, cycle exists
If fast reaches NULL, no cycle
Time: O(n), Space: O(1)

questions.question: Write a function to find the nth node from the end of a linked list in one pass.
keyPoints: Use two pointers technique
Move first pointer n nodes ahead
Move both pointers together
When first reaches end, second is at nth from end
Handle edge case: n > list length

questions.question: Compare the time complexities of insert and delete operations at different positions in a singly linked list.
keyPoints: Insert at beginning: O(1) - direct access
Insert at end: O(n) without tail, O(1) with tail pointer
Insert at position k: O(k) - must traverse
Delete from beginning: O(1) - direct access
Delete from end: O(n) - must find second-to-last
Delete from position k: O(k) - must traverse
